key_insight: The paper argues that LLM-based theorem provers must evolve from solving predefined formal problems to acting as research agents capable of discovering and rigorously formalizing frontier mathematics.
engineering_relevance: For computational science and engineering, this reframes AI4Math as an infrastructure challenge: building systems that integrate formal reasoning, exploration, tools, and human collaboration for open-ended research tasks.
method: Systematically reviews the AI4Math pipeline: datasets, auto-formalization, and proof synthesis.
Identifies gaps across relational structure, mathematical exploration, tool ecosystem, and human-AI collaboration.
Outlines a research roadmap toward research-grade formal mathematical agents.
evidence_signal: The paper is a position/review article rather than an empirical benchmark study, so its support comes from a structured survey of current system capabilities and limitations described in the abstract.
watch_item: A key implementation concern is that current theorem-proving systems are still optimized for well-specified tasks, making open-ended research settings difficult to evaluate and reliably formalize.
paper_title: From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier
